title: stellaria holostea var. apetala
intro: Stellaria holostea var. apetala, also known as greater stitchwort, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Greater stitchwort can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
